summary: Cinderella's mother tells her to stay kind and good before dying.
Cinderella's stepmother and stepsisters take her fine clothes and force her to work by the hearth.
Cinderella plants a hazel twig on her mother's grave, and a white bird begins granting her wishes.
The King announces a three-day festival where the Prince will seek a bride.
Birds help Cinderella sort lentils from the ashes, but her stepmother still refuses to let her attend.
Cinderella attends the first two nights of the festival in magical gowns and escapes before her family recognizes her.
On the third night, Cinderella loses a golden slipper on the palace stairs as she flees.
The Prince brings the slipper to Cinderella's home, and it fits Cinderella perfectly.
Cinderella forgives her stepfamily and marries the Prince.
